content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of task-gated, curiosity-pruned episodic memory in active learning agents maps directly to the human brain's active memory consolidation mechanism, where only uncertainty-triggered, high-information experiences are selected for long-term retention rather than storing all perceived input. This overlap suggests that cutting-edge artificial agent memory design is converging on a core principle that human memory evolved to implement naturally: memory itself is an active learning process, not just a passive storage buffer.
source_topics: memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
